{"key_event":"Breakup of Gran Colombia and constitutional New Granada","reasoning":"Gran Colombia’s collapse, fiscal exhaustion, demobilization, and persistent provincial power left the central state weak despite the more coherent 1832 New Granada institutions. Civilian constitutional rule, elections, press activity, municipal influence, and the decline of military dominance strengthened organized society, though mostly through elites and regional notables rather than broad mass participation.","society_change":0.9,"society_power":4.4,"state_change":-0.4,"state_power":3.1}
